runs.intro_text:
Dot Estimation Task
In this task, you'll see 6 groups of dots on each screen.
Your job is to give your best guess for how many dots are in each group.
The dots will appear briefly and then disappear.
Please do not count the dots. We want your quick visual estimate and intuition.
You'll start with one quick practice round first.
Enter whole numbers only.
Begin
Powered by Qualtrics
seen_texts: Welcome. Before taking part in this study, please read the consent form below and click the continue (arrow) button at the bottom of the page if you understand the statements and freely consent to participate in the study. The survey is being conducted by a group of researchers at Erasmus University, and it has been approved by the Erasmus University Internal Review Board (ETH2526-0361). Participation in the study typically takes around 10 minutes and is strictly anonymous. All responses are treated as confidential, and in no case will responses from individual participants be identified. Rather, all data will be pooled and published in aggregate form only. Participation is voluntary. If you have further questions about this study or your rights, or if you wish to lodge a concern, please contact schley@rsm.nl. | Powered by Qualtrics

In this task, you will estimate how wealth is distributed across five equally large groups of people in the United States. Each group represents 20% of the population, ordered from the richest to the poorest. | Wealth means total assets minus debts. This includes things like savings, investments, retirement accounts, businesses, and property, minus loans and other debts. | Please give your best estimates. You do not need to look anything up. | Use the full range of values if needed. Your answers do not need to be rounded to multiples of 5 or 10. | Powered by Qualtrics

Wealth share (%) for one group | The population is divided into five equal groups (each 20%), ordered from richest to poorest. | Group: Top 20% (richest) | What percentage of total wealth is owned by this group? | % | Powered by Qualtrics

Wealth share (%) for one group | The population is divided into five equal groups (each 20%), ordered from richest to poorest. | Group: Second 20% (next richest) | What percentage of total wealth is owned by this group? | % | Powered by Qualtrics

Wealth share (%) for one group | The population is divided into five equal groups (each 20%), ordered from richest to poorest. | Group: Middle 20% | What percentage of total wealth is owned by this group? | % | Powered by Qualtrics

Wealth share (%) for one group | The population is divided into five equal groups (each 20%), ordered from richest to poorest. | Group: Fourth 20% (second poorest) | What percentage of total wealth is owned by this group? | % | Powered by Qualtrics

Wealth share (%) for one group | The population is divided into five equal groups (each 20%), ordered from richest to poorest. | Group: Bottom 20% (poorest) | What percentage of total wealth is owned by this group? | % | Powered by Qualtrics

issues: numerosity_joint_brief: expected 36 total logged rows including practice, observed 47.
numerosity_joint_brief: expected 5 scored trial units, observed 10.

experiment.title: Combined Numerosity and Proportion Session
instructionsText: This session includes a few short judgment tasks.
Just follow the instructions on each screen and give your best estimate.
experiment.endText: The session is complete. If Qualtrics is collecting data, you may now continue in the survey.

blocks.name: Numerosity Practice
instructionsText: You will first complete a short dot-estimation practice block.

blocks.name: Proportion Practice
instructionsText: Next you will estimate percentages in a bar chart using open-ended numbers.
You'll see groups of dots on the screen.
Give your best estimate of how many dots there are.
Use your intuition and do not try to count.
numerosity.readyText: Get ready for the next dot display
numerosity.promptSeparate: How many dots are on the screen?
numerosity.promptJoint: How many dots are in each group?

instructionsText: You'll see bars split into colored sections.
Give your best guess for the percentage in each section.
Decimals are fine. Just go with what looks right.
proportion.jointPrompt: What percentage of the whole bar is each section?
proportion.jointHelperText: Type your best guess for A through E, and try to make them add up to 100. Decimals are fine.
proportion.separatePrompt: What percentage of the bar is shaded?
proportion.separateHelperText: Type your best guess from 0 to 100. Decimals are fine.
